item.title: Kamada's 89th-minute strike rescues Japan in thrilling draw with Netherlands
item.description: Japan's Daichi Kamada scored in the 89th minute to snatch a dramatic 2-all draw against the Netherlands in their Group F opener at the FIFA World Cup this morning. The Dutch took the lead through Virgil van Dijk before Japan responded with an equaliser from Keito Nakamura.
item.content:encoded:
Japan's Daichi Kamada scored in the 89th minute to snatch a dramatic 2-all draw against the Netherlands in their Group F opener at the FIFA World Cup this morning.

The Dutch took the lead through Virgil van Dijk before Japan responded with an equaliser from Keito Nakamura.

The Netherlands regained the advantage in the second half when Crysencio Summerville found the back of the net, putting his side on course for victory.

Japan continued to press for another equaliser and their persistence paid off when Kamada struck late to secure a valuable point.

The two sides went into the break locked at nill-all before the match burst into life in the second spell with four goals.

The result sees both teams open their Group F campaign with a point after an entertaining contest.

In other matches today, Ivory Coast will meet Ecuador at 11am while Sweden will play Tunisia at 2pm.

Stay with us for FIFA World Cup updates.

item.title: Du'Plessis Kirifi weighs in on Hurricanes’ inexperience ahead of final
item.description: Du'Plessis Kirifi weighs in on Hurricanes’ inexperience ahead of final

item.title: Luai scores a hattrick as Wests Tigers beat Titans 36-28
item.description: Navitalai Naivalurua · 15/06/2026 Jahrome Luai scored a hat-trick to help the Wests Tigers come from an 18-12 halftime deficit to beat the Gold Coast Titans 36-28 in the NRL last night.
item.content:encoded:
Navitalai Naivalurua · 15/06/2026

Jahrome Luai scored a hat-trick to help the Wests Tigers come from an 18-12 halftime deficit to beat the Gold Coast Titans 36-28 in the NRL last night.

The Tigers started strongly with tries from Starford To'a and Taylan May to take a 12-0 lead, but the Titans responded with three tries in six minutes through Jayden Campbell, Kurtis Morrin and Arama Hau to go into halftime ahead 18-12.

Beau Fermor extended the Titans' lead after crossing just two minutes into the second half before Luai responded for the Tigers to cut the deficit to 24-18.

Alex Seyfarth scored in the 53rd minute to level the scores at 24-all, but the Titans hit back through Phillip Sami to regain the lead at 28-24.

Luai crossed for his second try in the 70th minute to put the Tigers back in front before the 29-year-old completed his hat-trick in the 79th minute to seal a 36-28 victory.

item.title: Germany thrash FIFA World Cup debutants Curacao 7-1
item.description: Germany delivered a ruthless statement in their FIFA World Cup opener, thrashing debutants Curacao 7-1 in their Group E clash in Houston. The four-time world champions opened the scoring through Felix Nmecha in the 6th minute, but Curacao briefly shocked the crowd when Livano Comenencia equalised in the 21st minute to score the nation's first-ever FIFA World Cup goal.
item.content:encoded:
Germany delivered a ruthless statement in their FIFA World Cup opener, thrashing debutants Curacao 7-1 in their Group E clash in Houston.

The four-time world champions opened the scoring through Felix Nmecha in the 6th minute, but Curacao briefly shocked the crowd when Livano Comenencia equalised in the 21st minute to score the nation's first-ever FIFA World Cup goal.

Germany quickly regained control, with Nico Schlotterbeck restoring the lead in the 38th minute before Kai Havertz converted a penalty in first-half injury time to make it 3-1 at halftime.

Jamal Musiala extended Germany's advantage early in the second half before Nathaniel Brown added a fifth goal with a superb volley.

Deniz Undav then scored abother goal before Havertz grabbed his second goal of the match to complete the 7-1 rout.

Despite the heavy defeat, Curacao made history as the smallest nation by size and population ever to compete at a FIFA World Cup and celebrated a memorable moment through Comenencia's goal.

Germany's dominant victory gives them the perfect start to their Group E campaign.

In other matches, Japan will take on the Netherlands at 8am, Ecuador will face Ivory Coast at 11am, while Sweden will take on Tunisia at 2pm.

item.title: Japan and Netherlands eye crucial opening win at FIFA World Cup
item.description: Japan and the Netherlands will face each other in a crucial FIFA World Cup Group F clash, with both sides aiming to make a strong start to their campaign. Japan is making its eighth FIFA World Cup appearance and heads into the tournament after becoming the first nation to qualify through the Asian qualifiers.
item.content:encoded:
Japan and the Netherlands will face each other in a crucial FIFA World Cup Group F clash, with both sides aiming to make a strong start to their campaign.

Japan is making its eighth FIFA World Cup appearance and heads into the tournament after becoming the first nation to qualify through the Asian qualifiers.

The Samurai Blue reached the Round of 16 at the 2002, 2010, 2018 and 2022 World Cups and are currently ranked 18th in the FIFA World Rankings.

The Japanese will be hoping to build on their impressive showing at the 2022 World Cup in Qatar, where they stunned both Germany and Spain in the group stage.

Standing in their way is Netherlands, which is making its 12th FIFA World Cup appearance.

The Dutch have reached three World Cup finals but are still chasing their first title. They are currently ranked 8th in the FIFA World Rankings.

The Netherlands boasts a squad packed with talent playing in Europe's top leagues and will look to begin the tournament with a victory.

With both teams expected to challenge for a place in the knockout stages, the result could play a major role in shaping Group F.

Meanwhile, Germany delivered a ruthless statement in their FIFA World Cup opener, thrashing debutants Curacao 7-1.

In other matches, Ecuador will face Ivory Coast at 11am, while Sweden will take on Tunisia at 2pm.

item.title: Players to be investigated after referee assault - FRU
item.description: The Tavua Rugby player who hit the referee from a blind spot will be investigated and taken to task.This has been confirmed by the

item.title: Impressive Australia beat Turkey 2-0 to make strong World Cup start
item.description: Australia produced one of the upsets of the opening round of the 2026 FIFA World Cup, defeating Turkey 2-0 in their Group D clash in Vancouver this afternoon. The Socceroos took the lead in the 27th minute when rising star Nestory Irankunda burst through on a counterattack, beat his defender and calmly slotted home.
item.content:encoded:
Australia produced one of the upsets of the opening round of the 2026 FIFA World Cup, defeating Turkey 2-0 in their Group D clash in Vancouver this afternoon.

The Socceroos took the lead in the 27th minute when rising star Nestory Irankunda burst through on a counterattack, beat his defender and calmly slotted home.

Connor Metcalfe sealed the victory in the 75th minute with a superb long range strike.

Despite Turkey dominating possession and creating numerous chances, Australia's disciplined defence and goalkeeper Patrick Beach stood firm to secure all three points.

The result leaves Australia second in Group D after the United States opened their campaign with a convincing 4-1 win over Paraguay.

In other results today, Scotland beat Haiti 1-0, while Brazil and Morocco played out a 1-1 draw while Qatar and Switzerland also finished 1-1.

In tomorrow's matches, Germany will face Curacao at 5am, the Netherlands will take on Japan at 8am, Côte d'Ivoire will meet Ecuador at 11am while Sweden will play Tunisia at 2pm.

item.title: New York erupts in celebration as Knicks end 53-year NBA title drought
item.description: The New York Knicks have won their first National Basketball Association (NBA) championship in more than 50 years, defeating the San Antonio Spurs 94-90 in Game 5 of the Finals to clinch the best-of-seven series and spark celebrations across New York City. The victory marks the Knicks' first NBA title since 1973 and completes a remarkable turnaround for a franchise that has endured decades of disa
item.content:encoded:
The New York Knicks have won their first National Basketball Association (NBA) championship in more than 50 years, defeating the San Antonio Spurs 94-90 in Game 5 of the Finals to clinch the best-of-seven series and spark celebrations across New York City.

The victory marks the Knicks' first NBA title since 1973 and completes a remarkable turnaround for a franchise that has endured decades of disappointment. The team's last appearance in the NBA Finals came in 1999, when they were also beaten by the Spurs.

Although the title-clinching game was played in Texas, thousands of fans packed sports bars, public watch parties and city streets across New York to witness the historic moment.

Supporters described the atmosphere as electric, with many reflecting on the club's lengthy championship drought.

Ahead of the game, New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani announced that the city had partnered with the Knicks to host public watch parties outside Madison Square Garden, Radio City Music Hall and Wollman Rink.

In a social media post, Mamdani urged supporters to celebrate responsibly and look out for one another while enjoying the historic occasion.

The celebrations came despite reports of isolated incidents involving Spurs supporters in the lead-up to the game, including an assault that reportedly left one fan in hospital and another attack on a fast-food worker wearing a Spurs jersey.

For Knicks fans, however, the night was ultimately about ending a 53-year wait and witnessing a championship many believed they might never see.

item.title: Switzerland held to 1-1 draw by Qatar after late equaliser
item.description: Switzerland were held to a 1-1 draw by Qatar in their Group B match of the 2026 FIFA World Cup at the San Francisco Bay Area Stadi
